steps.voice: Peak Hold tracks the all-time max and min of every registered signal silently in the background. There's no enable, no toggle, no setup — the firmware has done it from the moment it booted. View Peaks opens the live table.

Live peak table. Every signal the dash sees gets one row — current, all-time min, all-time max — and the table refreshes about twice a second. The numbers stretch all the way back to whenever you last hit Reset, or to the last factory reset if you never have.

Watch the Max column on a session. Peak boost on a dyno run. Max coolant on a hot-soak after shutdown. Lowest battery voltage during cranking. The dash captures all of it whether or not a widget is bound to that signal — peak tracking lives in the signal layer, not the widget layer.
Reset All button up top wipes every peak and min back to the next sample's value. Useful at the start of a session — clear the lot, drive, then come back here to see the truth.

And this works alongside per-widget peak display. Any Panel widget has a Show Peak option in its config — Off, Max, Min, or Both — and renders a small secondary label under the main number. Watch peak boost while you drive, no scrolling required.
Back to settings. Peak Hold pairs nicely with the data logger right next to it — log a session to SD, then check the live peaks here without ever opening the CSV. Two ways to look at the same drive.
